reasoning: The informal statement asks for the minimum value of the expression for x > 0. The Lean statement formalizes this as sInf of the set of values of the expression over x > 0, equal to the solution (6). Since the expression simplifies to 3(x + 1/x) ≥ 6 with equality at x = 1, the minimum is actually attained, so sInf equals min for this set. Thus "sInf = 6" is equivalent to "the minimum value is 6". The set-builder notation correctly captures the range of the expression over x > 0, and this matches the standard Putnam formalization convention. No hypotheses are silently changed.
